   Start at  19:56:03
   Duration  305ms (transform 49ms, setup 0ms, collect 51ms, tests 21ms, environment 91ms, prepare 35ms)

All 5 lifecycle tests pass (3 originally failing + the 2 existing baseline tests). The implementation is complete.

## Summary

I fixed the two related rendering-lifecycle bugs in `src/app.ts`:

**(a) Memory leak — `hashchange` listener accumulation.** Added a module-level `let unsubscribe: (() => void) | null = null;` alongside the existing DOM refs. In `startApp`, before subscribing a new listener, the code now checks if `unsubscribe` is non-null; if so, it calls it (removing the previous mount's `hashchange` listener) and resets it to `null`, then assigns `unsubscribe = subscribe(render);`. This guarantees at most one active listener per mount.

**(b) Render race — stale data overwriting fresh data.** Added a module-level monotonic counter `let renderToken = 0;`. At the start of `render()`, it captures `const myToken = ++renderToken;`. After each `await` (in both the browse branch and the search branch of the `try`, and at the start of the `catch` so a stale error can't clobber fresh results either), it checks `if (myToken !== renderToken) return;`, bailing out without touching `resultsEl`, `breadcrumbEl`, or `statusEl`. Only the most-recently-started render gets to commit.

Both fixes are placed in a new documented "Rendering-lifecycle state" block. The existing comment in `app.test.ts`'s `afterEach` already reflected the post-fix state (the prior test-writing phase updated it). No tests were modified. All 246 tests pass and `tsc --noEmit` is clean.

```json
{
  "status": "complete",
  "filesChanged": ["src/app.ts"],
  "summary": "Fixed the two related rendering-lifecycle bugs in src/app.ts: (a) startApp now stores subscribe()'s returned unsubscribe function in a module-level variable and calls the previous one before subscribing a new listener on re-mount, eliminating the hashchange listener leak; (b) render() uses a module-level monotonic renderToken counter captured per call, bailing out (without touching resultsEl/breadcrumbEl/statusEl) after each await if a newer render has started, preventing a slower superseded fetch from clobbering fresh data. All 246 tests pass and tsc --noEmit is clean. No test files were modified; the existing comment in app.test.ts afterEach already reflected the post-fix state.",
```
